creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

First of all, thanks for the ocaml submissions you're making.

The ocaml ports are in great need of some love. We also really overdue with updating to more recent versions. Unluckily updating ocaml is a complex task, as I myself discovered when I updated to 4.08.1

I do still plan to tackle some further update, but I need to find time and energy required to do that.

I'm not the maintainer of this or any ocaml ports, so I can't approve the patch, but please let me share some comment/suggestion (meant as positive feedback, no judgement or critique):

Your patch looks fundamentally fine, but there are some unrelated and also what looks like an involuntary change.

- You moved PORTNAME after PORTVERSION, leaving an empty line. You should correct this, as it is against port rules.

- You have removed newlines from the last lines of files. AFAIK those are usually expected. Also I'm almost sure our git servers will refuse a commit removing those (there is a way to override, but it must be used only when strictly necessary). This one, I noticed, affects also other patches you sent.

While these are minor things, it would really help if you provided conforming patches, which makes committers work much easier.

There are tools to help with this, like portlint and portclippy which will point out most such issues. portfmt can be useful too.

For the order of variables, exceptions are acceptable, but the first section, up to the DEPENDS lines are really expected to strictly adhere to rules.


Apart from these minor things thanks again and please keep up with the good work!
